S&P 500, Nasdaq hit record highs on renewed AI bets, rate-cut hope (Reuters) -The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite hit all-time highs on Friday as renewed AI enthusiasm and the prospect of a looser monetary policy powered a recovery in U.S. stocks from months-long rout.  The benchmark index rose 0.59% to 6,178.80 points, surpassing the previous peak of 6,147.43 on February 19, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq gained 0.59% to 20,299.72 points, exceeding its record high of 20,204.58 on December 16.  Markets rallied this week as an upbeat forecast from  chipmaker Micron revived investor confidence on artificial intelligence, while Nvidia inched closer to  $4 trillion market capitalization after reclaiming its position as the world's most valuable company.
